title: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)
business_definition: MRR is the predictable monthly revenue from active subscriptions. MRR = sum of all active subscription amounts normalised to monthly. New MRR + Expansion MRR - Churned MRR - Contraction MRR = Net New MRR.
diagnostic_questions: Which MRR component drove the change — new, expansion, churn, or contraction?
Is ARR on track given current MRR growth rate?
What is the net revenue retention (NRR) — are existing customers expanding or contracting?
Which plan tier contributes most to MRR and how is that tier trending?

causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date) AS month, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'new' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS new_mrr, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'expansion' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS expansion_mrr, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'churn' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS churned_mrr, SUM(CASE WHEN event_type = 'contraction' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS contraction_mrr FROM mrr_events GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

causal_relationships.symptom: MRR growth slowing despite stable churn
causal_relationships.detection_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) AS month, COUNT(*) AS new_subscriptions, AVG(monthly_amount) AS avg_mrr_per_customer FROM subscriptions WHERE created_at >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

inflation_causes.cause: Annual contracts counted fully in first month instead of spreading monthly
inflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT billing_interval, COUNT(*), SUM(amount) FROM subscriptions WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY 1

deflation_causes.cause: Paused subscriptions excluded but still represent committed revenue
deflation_causes.detection_sql: SELECT status, SUM(monthly_amount) FROM subscriptions GROUP BY 1

anti_patterns: Do not add one-time charges to MRR — only include recurring amounts
Normalise all billing intervals to monthly before summing (annual / 12, quarterly / 3)

sql_assets.active_mrr: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN billing_interval = 'monthly' THEN amount WHEN billing_interval = 'annual' THEN amount / 12.0 WHEN billing_interval = 'quarterly' THEN amount / 3.0 ELSE amount END) AS mrr FROM subscriptions WHERE status = 'active'
